summary: Question 4.31 from arXiv:2401.06062 asks for a characterization of when the tensor product $\prod_{i=1}^d K_{n_i}$ (vertices are $d$-tuples, adjacent iff differing in every coordinate) is prime. The source paper was published in the Journal of Combinatorics (2026, vol. 17, no. 2, pp. 223–252), where the question appears as an open problem. No follow-up paper specifically resolving this combinatorial question was found in a targeted literature search.
search_queries: prime Cayley graphs tensor product complete graphs K_n primality 2401.06062
Kronecker product complete graphs prime graph Chudnovsky Spirkl 2024

notes: The source paper itself was published in the Journal of Combinatorics (2026). Question 4.31 arises as the combinatorial core of the semisimple ring case of a broader open problem (Question 4.28) about primality of ring Cayley graphs. No follow-up paper resolving or partially resolving this question was found after 5 web queries.
